item.title: U.S. Senate passes housing bill carrying a four-year ban on Federal Reserve issuance of a central bank digital currency.
EU MiCA transitional period ends July 1; only 194 of 3,000+ registered crypto firms have secured licenses across the bloc.
Greece's regulator expected to deny Binance's EU MiCA license application ahead of the June 30 deadline, per Reuters.
Franklin Templeton finalizes acquisition of digital asset manager 250 Digital, forming a new business unit called Franklin Crypto.
Q2 2026 becomes the most-hacked quarter on record with 83 exploits and $755 million stolen, led by bridge vulnerabilities.
EBA updates Travel Rule guidelines to extend anti-money-laundering requirements to crypto-asset service providers under MiCA.
Taiko halts its Ethereum layer-2 network after an attacker exploited a bridge vulnerability to steal $1.7 million.
Kraken obtains MiCA license through Ireland's Central Bank, securing continued EU market access after the July 1 deadline.
JPMorgan moves to accept crypto assets as loan collateral following enactment of the GENIUS Act stablecoin legislation.
JPMorgan, Mastercard, and Ripple complete cross-border tokenized Treasury settlement using XRP Ledger infrastructure.
Citigroup plans institutional bitcoin custody launch in 2026, integrating digital assets into unified safekeeping and reporting.
MoneyGram joins Solana as a proof-of-stake validator, expanding the payments firm's stablecoin settlement infrastructure.
Morgan Stanley's partnership with Galaxy Digital enables bitcoin as collateral for institutional lending portfolios.
Citi research puts global tokenized assets at $17 billion today, with a 2030 bull-case projection of $8.2 trillion.
